methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Captura, a Series A climate and energy company founded by Harry Atwater and CX Xiang, has raised $30M+ to date. Valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (LNZA), including same-stage players Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $30M raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $60M. Peer-median analysis across the three same-sector, same-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $150M, with a range of $110M to $200M. These methods anchor the valuation at materially different levels, reflecting uncertainty in both company-specific execution risk and peer comparable relevance.

Synthesizing both approaches produces a triangulated range of $51M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $105M. The wide spread reflects tension between the conservative stage-progression method and peer multiples that may embed higher growth expectations or stronger IP positions than Captura currently demonstrates. Key uncertainties include the maturity of Captura's core technology relative to named peers, capital efficiency in path to Series B, and durability of the peer comp set in a rapidly evolving sector. Additional diligence on technology differentiation and customer traction would help narrow this range materially.
